The second story is about love and loss, which changed everything for me.
I dropped out of Reed College after the first six months but stayed around another eighteen.
My third story is about death, and how remembering it clarifies every big choice.
Stay hungry and stay foolish, and never settle for living someone else's dogma.
Calligraphy classes taught me about serif and sans serif typefaces ten years before the Mac.
Getting fired from Apple was the best thing that could have happened to me.
Remembering that you are going to die is the best way to avoid the trap of thinking you have something to lose.
No one wants to die, and yet death is the destination we all share.
